title: stachys cuneata
intro: Stachys cuneata, also known as the wedgeleaf hedge nettle, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family Lamiaceae. It is native to North America, particularly in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, and has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Stachys cuneata include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as acting as an antibacterial agent to fight off infections. In herbal preparations, Stachys cuneata is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically in the form of salves or ointments to treat various skin conditions.
